vehicles.slot: DNA rotary selector — override button/slot under console near selector
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
The Giulia uses an 8-speed ZF automatic with a DNA rotary selector — locate the shift lock override slot at the base of the rotary selector under the center console trim.
Use a trim pry tool or fingernail to remove the console trim piece near the selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the override slot and press firmly.
While holding the override, press the brake pedal.
Rotate the DNA dial to N. Confirm N on the instrument cluster.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
12V battery is under the hood — connect jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and rotate the DNA dial to N normally.
RWD: lift from front — rear (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD (Q4): FLATBED ONLY.
EPB must be released before towing — hold EPB button with 12V power. Without power: EPB requires direct 12V to the actuator motor at the rear caliper.
vehicles.notes: RWD standard, AWD (Q4) optional. AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: AWD Q4: flatbed preferred. Italian vehicle — connector and trim may be less familiar.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front (rear drive wheels roll). AWD Q4: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: ZF 8HP — same transmission as BMW/Land Rover. Well-proven.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button. Manual: run 12V directly to EPB motor to rewind, or remove motor and wind back manually (requires tools).
steps: Manual (no power): remove EPB actuator motor. Wind manually, or apply 12V directly to motor terminals to retract (polarity selects direction).

battery.warnings: AWD Q4: flatbed preferred. EPB manual release requires motor removal.

knownIssues.title: EPB Manual Release Requires Motor Access
knownIssues.detail: Without 12V power, the Giulia's EPB cannot be released without physically accessing the EPB actuator motor at the rear caliper and either directly powering it (12V to motor terminals) or manually winding the piston back. A jump start is strongly preferred.

vehicles.slot: DNA rotary selector — override near selector base under console trim
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
The Stelvio uses an 8-speed ZF automatic — same DNA rotary selector as the Giulia.
Locate the shift lock override at the base of the rotary selector under the center console trim.
Insert key or flathead into the override slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the override.
Rotate the selector to N. Confirm N on the instrument cluster.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
12V battery under the hood — connect jump pack.
Once powered, press brake and rotate DNA dial to N normally.
All Stelvio models are AWD (Q4) — FLATBED ONLY.
EPB must be released before loading — hold EPB button with 12V power. Manual EPB release requires access to the rear caliper EPB motor.
vehicles.notes: Crossover version of Giulia platform. Same drivetrain rules.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from front. AWD Q4: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: Same EPB and towing rules as Giulia.

epb.override: Restore 12V. Hold EPB button. Manual: EPB motor access at rear caliper.
steps: Manual: EPB motor direct power or physical wind-back.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — override slot near base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
The Tonale has an electronic selector — locate the override slot near the selector base under the console trim.
Remove any trim cap. Insert key or flathead and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the override.
Move the selector to N. Confirm N on the display.
PHEV variant: restore 12V power first — the PHEV electronic system requires 12V to shift.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
12V battery under the hood — connect jump pack.
Once powered, shift to N normally.
PHEV AWD: FLATBED ONLY — 12V auxiliary only, never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
MHEV FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground.
vehicles.notes: PHEV AWD: flatbed only, 12V only. MHEV FWD: rear lift.
vehicles.warn: PHEV AWD: FLATBED ONLY. MHEV: 12V mild hybrid — no HV danger but note 48V system on some.
vehicles.towMethod: MHEV FWD: lift from rear (front drive wheels roll). PHEV AWD: FLATBED ONLY.

vehicles.slot: Small cap or slot near selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the small cap near the selector base on the center console.
Pry off cap with a key or thin flathead.
Insert key into the slot and press firmly.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: FWD luxury sedan. Rare — manual may be more common on US models found.
vehicles.warn: Rare vehicle — unfamiliar connectors and trim. Consult owner manual if available.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: Rare vehicle — be cautious with trim removal.

vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Manual transmission: depress clutch, select Neutral. Rare in US — mostly gray market or private imports. Parts availability is limited.
vehicles.warn: Very rare in US market. High value — flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. Flatbed preferred due to vehicle value and rarity.
towNotes: High-value, low-production vehicle — flatbed strongly preferred to avoid damage.

vehicles.drivetrain: RWD or AWD (Q4 on some Brera trims)
vehicles.slot: Small cap near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock release: small cap near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap with small flathead.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, shift to Neutral normally.
Selespeed (automated manual): requires 12V power to disengage — jump battery first.
vehicles.notes: Selespeed is an automated manual (robotized), NOT a true torque-converter automatic. It requires electrical power to shift. Very rare in the US.
vehicles.warn: SELESPEED: Must have 12V power to shift — jump pack required. High value — flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. AWD (Q4): flatbed only. Flatbed strongly preferred for all variants.
towNotes: RWD variants: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels up.
Q4 AWD variants: FLATBED ONLY — cannot disengage AWD.
High-value vehicle — use flatbed with padded wheel grids.

knownIssues.title: Selespeed Cannot Shift Without Power
knownIssues.detail: The Selespeed transmission is an automated manual gearbox controlled by electrohydraulic actuators. Without 12V power it will not disengage from gear. Always jump the 12V battery before attempting any shift override.

battery.warnings: Selespeed: must restore 12V before attempting shift.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near selector base on center console — TCT requires power
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
TCT dual-clutch: requires 12V power to shift — jump battery first.
Locate the shift lock slot near the selector base on the center console.
With power restored, press brake and use the paddle shifters or selector to move to Neutral.
If using mechanical slot without power: insert thin flathead, press firmly, hold while pressing brake, move to N.
TCT without power will strongly resist — apply steady firm pressure for several seconds.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — battery is in the REAR of the vehicle, in the engine compartment at the rear.
4C is MID-ENGINE: the 'hood' at the front is a frunk (storage). Battery is at the rear engine bay.
Once powered, use TCT paddle shifters or selector to select Neutral.
Flatbed strongly preferred — high value, low clearance, mid-engine.
vehicles.notes: Mid-engine. Battery is in the REAR engine compartment — not the front frunk. Carbon fiber body — use extreme care with straps. Very low clearance.
vehicles.warn: Battery in REAR engine bay, NOT the front. Mid-engine carbon body — flatbed ONLY with padded equipment.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD — FLATBED ONLY. Extremely low clearance and carbon fiber body make wheel-lift dangerous.
towNotes: FLATBED ONLY — very low clearance, mid-engine, carbon fiber body.
Battery is at the REAR in the engine compartment — open the rear hood, not the front frunk.
Use flatbed with padded wheel ramps only. Carbon fiber body can crack if improperly loaded.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Rear Engine Bay
knownIssues.detail: The 4C is mid-engine. The front 'hood' opens to a small storage frunk. The 12V battery is in the REAR engine compartment. Many operators open the front and find no battery — open the REAR hood.

knownIssues.title: TCT Requires Power to Shift
knownIssues.detail: The 6-speed TCT (Twin Clutch Transmission) is electro-hydraulic and will not release without 12V power. Always jump the battery before attempting to select Neutral.

battery.location: REAR engine compartment — open the rear hood, NOT the front frunk
battery.warnings: Battery is in the REAR. Carbon fiber body — flatbed only, padded equipment required.
